comparison_use: baseline source family already embedded in current framework
checks whether five-paragraph orders and common terminology can stay as a multinational contract
checks whether doctrine, command, decision, and terminology assumptions are not US-only
checks that role authority, culture, and information governance are expressed as localizable controls
checks that command authority, reporting culture, and legal analogies remain jurisdiction-gated

policy_findings.us_assumption: S2/S3/S4/S6 staff codes are treated as if they were universal staff labels.
policy_findings.comparative_finding: NATO, UK, Canadian, and Korean public materials share command, staff, and function concepts, but exact staff labels and rank/appointment names vary by force.
policy_findings.resolution: Keep S2/S3/S4/S6 as internal function IDs only and require an alias map when adapting the framework to a non-US organization.

verification.method: Compared current role policy with NATO/UK terminology sources and non-US public defence organization pages.

policy_findings.us_assumption: Mission command autonomy could be read as blanket decentralized execution.
policy_findings.comparative_finding: All reviewed source families support clear authority, intent, and accountability, but the degree of decentralization and retained decisions must be locally set.
policy_findings.resolution: Express autonomy as authority-bounded execution: local doctrine may change terminology, but Commander/user retains scope, release, risk, and irreversible decisions.

verification.method: Checked authority controls against US authorities, UK defence doctrine, NATO allied doctrine, and Korean legal/source anchors.

policy_findings.us_assumption: The OPORD five-paragraph sequence is the only valid order shape.
policy_findings.comparative_finding: STANAG/NATO and UK doctrine support standardized orders and terminology, but national supplements can vary.
policy_findings.resolution: Keep the five-paragraph order as the normalized runtime contract while allowing source-doctrine aliases and annex naming differences.

verification.method: Compared STANAG 2014, NATO AJP-01, and UK joint doctrine publication anchors.

policy_findings.us_assumption: ROE/legal support analogies can be applied uniformly across jurisdictions.
policy_findings.comparative_finding: Legal authority, service law, and release constraints are jurisdiction-specific even when the operational need for constraints is common.
policy_findings.resolution: Treat ROE as a tool-use control analogy only. Any legal, public release, personal data, or real-world operational decision must route to local jurisdiction review and Commander/user approval.

verification.method: Compared US legal support anchors with Korean law source anchors and non-US official defence policy pages.
evidence: docs/source-map.md legal and Korean public source rows

policy_findings.us_assumption: DOTMLPF-P and US force management terminology could be read as the only lifecycle model.
policy_findings.comparative_finding: Other source families support capability, authority, readiness, documentation, and sustainment concerns, but not always the same US force-management vocabulary.
policy_findings.resolution: Keep DOTMLPF-P as a US-derived checklist and require the neutral label capability lifecycle review for multinational use.

verification.method: Compared force management policy with non-US command, organization, and public policy anchors.

policy_findings.us_assumption: USSOCOM SOF Truths are universal special operations doctrine.
policy_findings.comparative_finding: The SOF Truths are US source material; their safe AI value is as high-risk task-force heuristics, not as universal military doctrine.
policy_findings.resolution: Keep the AI SOF TF model as a US-derived high-risk TF pattern and require local doctrine review before naming it as another force's special operations model.

verification.method: Checked current SOF policy source anchors and compared them with the broader multinational source review.
evidence: docs/source-map.md AI special operations task force row

source_map: Add NATO/UK/Canada/Korea source family rows and local artifact row.
compendium: Add multinational doctrine consistency review notes and open research gaps.
